notes: Lesson 2 Part 3 (act 52, "Posterior reads the positive pile", 280.1s, 14 cues rescaled to the real MP3). Payoff of the Bayes arc: the posterior is read off the POSITIVE PILE only, P(sick|+)=TP/(TP+FP). In the shared 100-person world (prior 1%, sensitivity 99%, specificity 95%) the positive pile is 1 TP + 5 FP, so posterior = 1/6 ~= 17% despite a '99% accurate' test. Reuses bayes.py idioms (positive_pile, posterior_ratio, posterior_numbers, confusion_matrix, PopGrid, prior_tree, chip, stat_row). Single clean -ql review pass: READ start/mid/end frames for all 14 cues. Per-cue checklist (formula legibility+correctness, highlighted element matches narration, contrast, nothing off-frame/overlapping, chunk timing): all pass; no fixes needed (scenes authored following the vetted act10/act11 pattern, self.guard() safe-area assertion passed for every cue at render time). Timing verified in render log: every cue within pinning tolerance (Δ<=0.04s), final 279.6s vs audio 280.1s Δ0.5s. Cue13 end-frame lands past video end (41 frames extracted); cue13_mid shows the complete posterior math = 1/(1+5) = 1/6 ~= 17%. Concrete model shared with Parts 1 & 2: 100 people, prior 1%, sensitivity 99%, specificity 95%, TP1/FP5/FN0/TN94.
